step_by_step_thinking: \boxed{B} The evidence explicitly states that "Pimavanserin is an atypical antipsychotic" and highlights its unique mechanism—acting primarily as a 5HT2A receptor antagonist/inverse agonist without dopamine D2 receptor blockade—which distinguishes it from typical antipsychotics that block D2 receptors and frequently cause extrapyramidal side effects. Since typical antipsychotics are defined by their D2-blocking properties, pimavanserin’s absence of dopaminergic activity confirms it is not a typical antipsychotic.
raw_response:
\boxed{B} The evidence explicitly states that "Pimavanserin is an atypical antipsychotic" and highlights its unique mechanism—acting primarily as a 5HT2A receptor antagonist/inverse agonist without dopamine D2 receptor blockade—which distinguishes it from typical antipsychotics that block D2 receptors and frequently cause extrapyramidal side effects. Since typical antipsychotics are defined by their D2-blocking properties, pimavanserin’s absence of dopaminergic activity confirms it is not a typical antipsychotic.
\boxed{B}
